EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust estimated at approximately 61 kt (70 mph) struck a warehouse in northeast Reno.  According to newspaper reports, portions of the roof were torn off.  One piece of the roof landed on an automobile.  Another section of the roof landed next to a truck 30 feet away from the building.  A third section of the roof landed near a fence 50 feet away.  Fire sprinkler lines in the warehouse were also broken.
A rope like tornado was observed and photographed over open country about 1 mile west of the Eureka Airport. No reports of damage were received.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Portions of the road to the Tabor Creek Campground were washed out by floodwaters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: River flooding occurred in southern Lyon County during the latter half of May.  Areas along both the mainstem Walker River and the West Walker River experienced flooding.  The main flooding occurred in agricultural and lowland areas.  Basements and yards were flooded in the Mason and Yerington areas, and residents were forced to use sandbags to protect their property.  Farm buildings were also affected by the flooding in Mason.

Areas along the Humboldt River in Pershing County experienced minor to moderate flooding during the entire month of May.  Flooding occurred mainly in agricultural and lowland areas and affected pasture, cropland, irrigation structures, and rural roads.  Above Rye Patch Reservoir the Tungsten Mine Road bridge near Mill City was closed due to flooding.  Part of the lower campground below Rye Patch Dam was flooded, and the Derby-Big Five Road in the lower Humboldt valley was closed.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: One drowning at Lake Mead National Recreation Area.  7YO female, who was not wearing a personal flotation device and did not know how to swim, fell off a raft near Boulder Beach, NV.  Strong winds were a contributing factor.

A wind gust estimated at 39 kt (45 mph) caused damage near Los Altos Parkway in Sparks.  The Reno Gazette-Journal reported that a few fences were knocked down by the strong winds.  One homeowner had six feet of wood fence destroyed, along with two screen-glass doors that were shattered.

A 16 year old boy was struck and killed by lightning when he and his dad got out of their pickup truck to check the tires. His father was thrown to the ground when the lightning struck. The father was taken to the hospital for treatment and released. The boy's mother and sister stayed in the truck and were unharmed.
Heavy rainfall caused flash flooding along Interstate 80 between Lockwood and Reno.  Nevada Highway Patrol reported 2 feet of standing water on the interstate with only one lane open for traffic.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A record-breaking heat wave affected the eastern Sierra and western Nevada during the last two weeks of July.  The main area affected was western Nevada where over 50 high temperature records, and warm minimum temperature records, were either tied or broken at various locations throughout the latter half of the month.  As in past heat waves, temperatures during the afternoon at valley locations across western Nevada rose to above 100 degrees.  However, it was the warm nighttime temperatures that made this heat wave nearly unprecedented.  These warm nighttime readings in Reno might have been exacerbated by the urban heat island effect that the city has been experiencing during at least the last decade.  But smaller towns across the region also experienced very warm nighttime temperatures. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific storm moved through the central Sierra and western Nevada. This moist system dropped over a foot of snow in the Sierra and freezing rain in parts of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Icy conditions along Interstate 80 in Churchill County contributed to numerous traffic accidents.  A woman was killed in an accident when her automobile hit a slick section of I-80.  Her car went off the road and overturned approximately 17 miles east of Fernley just inside the Churchill County line.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous traffic accidents occurred on Interstate 80 in Churchill County the day after a winter storm left slick conditions across the region.  Late on the morning of December 22nd two vehicles overturned on I-80 near mile markers 83 and 87.  Two other accidents occurred at mile markers 60 and 61.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system over northwest Arizona brought strong north winds to much of southern Nevada and southeast California on December 27th and 28th.  The winds were much higher in the town of Beatty, NV than in surrounding areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind damage in Beatty, NV from non-thunderstorm winds.  Damage included roofs ripped off buildings, a large camping trailer blown over, an 800 gallon fiberglass water tank moved 300-400 yards, and an 8-by-10 foot storage shed with lumber stacked inside it flipped over a fence.  The weather station by the post office recorded a top wind speed of 45 mph, but the anemometer is sheltered from north winds.  An anemometer on a resident's roof recorded a peak gust of 87.5 mph.  The beginning and ending times of the high winds are estimates.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong cold front moving through eastern Elko county brought high winds that caused some damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds behind a strong cold front gusted to 66 mph at West Wendover.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong cold front moving through eastern Elko county brought high winds that caused some damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downslope winds off the Ruby Mountains associated with a strong cold front produced wind gusts to 83 mph at the Ruby Valley RAWS. A home in Ruby Valley was completely destroyed by the winds. The winds also moved a pickup truck 20 feet.
